steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The task is to explain what 'plosive' means using the notes. The answer must define the term itself, not describe a single phoneme like /p/ in isolation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The phoneme /p/, a unit of sound in which air flow is at first fully blocked, has labial articulation.": Frames /p/'s labial articulation as the main point, making “plosive” a parenthetical descriptor of one phoneme rather than the concept being explained — the term is not defined. ✗
  • "The phoneme /p/ in the word "pan" is plosive and is produced using both lips.": Labels /p/ as plosive and describes its articulation but never defines what plosive means — gives a specific instance rather than explaining the category. ✗
  • "The linguistic term "fricative" is used for units of sound in which air flow is partially blocked.": Defines fricative, not plosive — wrong term entirely. ✗
  • "In linguistics, plosive is a term used for units of sound in which air flow is at first fully blocked.": Directly defines the term using the key feature from the notes — complete airflow stoppage followed by release. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
